# Welcome {.unnumbered}

If you have ever stared at a spreadsheet and wondered, *"What is this data actually telling me?"* — then this book is for you.

Welcome to the **ETF2121 / ETF5921 Handbook (1st Edition)**: a practical, hands-on guide to the data analysis skills that matter most in the real world. Whether you are a student stepping into data for the very first time, a working professional looking to sharpen your toolkit, or simply a curious mind who wants to make sense of numbers, you are in the right place.

## What this book is about

Data is everywhere — in the businesses we run, the decisions we make, and the stories we tell. But raw data, on its own, says very little. The real magic happens when we learn how to *ask the right questions*, *pull out the right information*, and *communicate what we find* in a way that helps others make better decisions.

This handbook is built around that journey. Together, we will:

- **Query and shape data** using SQL — the language of databases.
- **Explore and visualise** patterns using R, a free and powerful tool used by analysts and researchers worldwide.
- **Build dashboards** in Power BI that turn raw numbers into clear, compelling stories.
- **Apply modelling techniques** to real-world problems — from forecasting demand to understanding customer behaviour.

Every chapter is written with one promise in mind: **no prior experience required**. If you can read, click, and copy a line of code, you can do this.

## How to read this book

This is not a textbook you read once and shelve. It is designed as a *companion*: short enough to follow during a busy semester, deep enough to return to long after the course is over. Each chapter offers:

- A clear walkthrough of the key ideas, in plain language.
- Worked examples using real datasets — not toy problems.
- Hands-on exercises that mirror the kinds of questions you will face at work.
- A summary and further reading for those who want to go deeper.

Every tool used in this book is **free and open-source**. You will never need to buy a licence to follow along. All you need is a laptop, an internet connection, and a willingness to experiment.

## A note from the author

I wrote this book for the student I once was — curious, a little overwhelmed, and unsure where to start. My hope is that by the final page, you will not only have the technical skills to analyse data confidently, but also the mindset to keep learning long after this course ends.

Mistakes are part of the journey, both yours and mine. If you spot one in these pages, or have an idea for how to make the book better, I would love to hear from you. Please reach out at [joan.tan@monash.edu](mailto:joan.tan@monash.edu).

Thank you for reading, and welcome aboard.

Yours sincerely,

**Dr. Joan Tan**
Senior Lecturer, Monash University

---

*This book is solely authored by Dr. Joan Tan and is affiliated with Monash University. It is licensed under a [Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International License](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/), which allows you to share and adapt the material for non-commercial purposes, provided you give appropriate credit and license your contributions under the same terms.*

&copy; 2026 Joan Tan. All rights reserved.
